What's going on everyone? There's three main steps to weigh-in day. All right, so the first one is the tech meal plan stats, recording, etc. This is kind of the administrative component of the weigh-in. You want to make sure they get set up, they feel comfortable, they understand how to use the systems that you use, all that kind of jazz. The order that I'm going to show you is the one that has seemed to work best for us at my facilities and the many, many, many other gym owners who have rocked this. So I'm going to show you two setups for the first part because the six-week challenge is a little bit different in terms of the meal plan setup. So it will change this initial structure and then step two and three will be the same for both the six-week and the 42-day. Most of the plans will, it depends on whether we have a long plan or a short plan. If it's an internal plan, it tends to be a little bit shorter, but basically you need to be able to use your judgment here. The single page type plans is going to be following this model that I'm showing you. The next model that I'll show you is kind of the long book, huge recipe book type plans. And in those cases, you'll use the alternative one. So this is for the short plan. The first thing they do when they walk in, you'll double check their contract. I can't tell you how many times we've had. I've done this a zillion times and every single time there's still someone who has something wrong with their contract. They didn't sign it or they did an initial something or they put the wrong card down or one of their payments didn't go through before the start date. Whatever it is, have a stack of the problem children right there. So as soon as they walk in, you can either have a list of the names or you can have the contract sitting there either way to do. And then have your assistant just double check to make sure that they are not on the special snowflake list. If they are, then have them fix their contract issue right there and then before beginning. And then that takes you to the next step. So next step is as soon as they walk in the door, you make sure they're not on the naughty list. You step them right on the scales, put your scale right by the door so they can get going, put it separate from where you're doing the selling so that it doesn't interfere with sales. So they'll hop on the scale, make sure they take their shoes off or keep their, I mean whatever you want to do, just disrobe themselves accordingly. Then you'll have them, you'll give them the meal plan that's according to their weight. So in this one, you know, in the six week challenge or the individual plans, there's typically a breakdown of like if you're between this weight and this weight, you get this plan, that's the plan you're going to select for them and you're going to write their weight on the top. So you're going to write it in two places, one on the actual plan and the second on your clipboard, which is going to have the weekly weigh in stats. So this will be under week zero. You will write where you will write their weight down there and that's going to be the official clipboard for the gym. So they're going to have one so they know where it is and then you're going to have your official copy that you're going to hang on the wall once the day is done. After that, you'll give them the omron, they'll measure their body fat percentage. Again you'll record it both on the plan and on the clipboard. After that, you'll have them download the app, whatever app, most people have some sort of app that they're using for their gym, for scheduling or for accountability for whatever it is, whatever apps you need them to download. This is the time to do it, make sure that they get set up with it so that everything's hunky dory and to make sure that no one has any tech issues. So usually we would do steps one through six together, make sure everyone's got that. So if you have two people that are coming in for that next moment, make sure they both do one through six and then we move to the downloading of tech stuff after that. Then I make sure that they friend me on Facebook and friend whoever their accountability coach is on Facebook and that way it makes it easy for us. This is an FYI for your staff. I would highly recommend you create an email for them and give them an official Facebook that is through the business so that if for some reason down the road they go AWOL, you can change the password and you would own that profile instead of them being able to direct message all of your clients. Just a little tidbit. Next one, after they have friended you, you're going to take them to the page and show them how to check in and that will be their first official check in of the six week challenge You can usually use that as a referral opportunity and say, hey, if you want, put this link on your post and I'll love you 10 times more and usually they'll say how awesome we are. Just make it a joke. And there, just like that, you got to like on your business page and you got your first check in and you're going to explain to them that for the duration of the challenge every time they come in, you want them to check in on Facebook and that's just for referral marketing. Okay, so that concludes the short style meal plans like the six week challenge, not exhaustive of the six week challenge but the short style meal plans. Alright, so the next one I'm going to break down is the longer style meal plans.
